creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.
contentSnippet: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.
contentSnippet: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

title: (No AI) The ChatGPT browser is already dead
author: Jay Peters
content:
![App icon for Chat GPT Atlas in front of a graphic blue background.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/10/STKB372_CHAT_GPT_ATLAS_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced [in October](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/803475/openais-ai-powered-browser-chatgpt-atlas-google-chrome-competition-agent), but as part of its wave of news [about ChatGPT Work today](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/963464/openai-gpt-5-6-codex-chatgpt-work), the company confirmed that it will be "sunsetting" Atlas and is targeting an August 9th date for deprecation.

In recent months, OpenAI has also shut down the [video generation app Sora](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899850/openai-sora-ai-chatgpt) and paused plans for a [ChatGPT "adult mode"](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/901293/openai-adult-mode-erotic-chatbot-shelved-indefinitely) as it works to [reduce "side quests"](http://reddit.com/) and catch up with Anthropic [on productivity features](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/911118/openai-memo-cro-ai-competition-anthropic).

In [a thread about](https://x.com/JamesZmSun/status/2075290224327057644?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw%7Ctwcamp%5Etweetembed%7Ctwterm%5E2075290224327057644%7Ctwgr%5E6c8e130455b5aaa9840a043d94cfd131c19e96d8%7Ctwcon%5Es1_&ref_url=https%3A%2F%2F9to5mac.com%2F2026%2F07%2F09%2Fopenai-is-discontinuing-chatgpt-atlas-its-standalone-desktop-browser%2F) the ChatGPT Work announcements, which includes an updated browser in the desktop …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/963654/openai-chatgpt-atlas-ai-browser-shut-down-sunset)
contentSnippet:
OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced in October, but as part of its wave of news about ChatGPT Work today, the company confirmed that it will be "sunsetting" Atlas and is targeting an August 9th date for deprecation.
In recent months, OpenAI has also shut down the video generation app Sora and paused plans for a ChatGPT "adult mode" as it works to reduce "side quests" and catch up with Anthropic on productivity features. 
In a thread about the ChatGPT Work announcements, which includes an updated browser in the desktop …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Google will now tell you if an ad was made with AI
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![A screenshot of an AI disclosure label on a Google ad](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/google-ai-ads-label.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

You can see if ads on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube were [made or edited using AI](https://blog.google/products/ads-commerce/google-ads-ai-transparency-labels/) from a new section in Google's "My Ad Center," as reported earlier by [_TechCrunch_](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/09/google-will-now-disclose-which-ads-are-made-with-ai/). The update, announced on Thursday, adds a "created or edited with AI" label under the "how this ad was made" tab. Users can find it by tapping the three dots or info button on ads, which pulls up the same panel where you can also block or report ads.

Google says it will automatically apply the AI label to any ads made with its own generative AI advertising tools, but AI ads made elsewhere will need to have the label applied manually. In some regions, Google's new AI …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/963628/google-ai-generated-ads-label)

Google says it will automatically apply the AI label to any ads made with its own generative AI advertising tools, but AI ads made elsewhere will need to have the label applied manually. In some regions, Google's new AI  …
